Banco di Desio e della Brianza (BIT:BDB) has drawn fresh attention after reporting fourth quarter net income of €21.41 million and declaring an annual dividend of €0.5105 per share for 2026.

See our latest analysis for Banco di Desio e della Brianza.

The recent dividend announcement and fourth quarter earnings update come after a mixed price pattern, with a 1-day share price return of 2.24% at €8.69 contrasting with a 7.36% year to date decline. At the same time, a 1-year total shareholder return of 19.43% and a roughly 3x total shareholder return over five years suggest long term momentum has been strong even as short term sentiment has cooled.

If this bank’s move has you thinking about what else might be interesting in the market, it could be a good time to broaden your search with 104 top founder-led companies.

With Banco di Desio e della Brianza offering a €0.5105 dividend per share and reporting fourth quarter net income of €21.41 million, you might be wondering whether the current share price is leaving upside on the table or already reflecting future growth.

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 9x: Is it justified?

On a simple earnings basis, Banco di Desio e della Brianza trades on a P/E of 9x at a last close of €8.69, which is lower than both the Italian market and banking peers.

P/E compares the current share price to the bank’s earnings per share, so a lower P/E can suggest the market is assigning a lower price tag to each euro of profit. For a bank that has reported net income of €127.309 million and has grown earnings by 22.4% per year over the past 5 years, that sets an interesting reference point for readers assessing how much future earnings strength is already in the price.

Relative to the Italian market P/E of 17.5x, BDB’s 9x multiple is considerably lower, and it is also below both the peer average of 10.3x and the wider European banks average of 10.8x. That combination means the shares are priced at a discount to earnings across several comparison sets, even though earnings growth over the past year of 3.9% has been slower than the bank’s own 5 year average and below the 8.7% reported for the banks industry.

However, you still need to keep an eye on the recent 7.36% year to date share price decline and any shifts in earnings that could challenge this lower P/E case.

Another view from our DCF model

The P/E of 9x suggests Banco di Desio e della Brianza could be on the cheap side, but our DCF model offers a different angle. On that basis, the current share price of €8.69 sits above an estimated future cash flow value of €8.32, which points to the shares looking slightly overvalued instead. data.title: How Mixed 2025 Results and AI Test Wins At Cohu (COHU) Have Changed Its Investment Story

  • Alongside these mixed results, management guided first‑quarter 2026 sales to about US$122 million and highlighted a sharp systems order rebound, growing high-bandwidth‑memory test exposure, and expected gross‑margin improvement driven by recurring revenue and new Eclipse and AI‑related design wins.

To own Cohu, you need to believe that its mix of systems and recurring test revenue can eventually support healthier margins despite current losses. The latest quarter reinforced that tension: sales grew, bookings and HBM exposure improved, but the loss was wider than expected. Management’s emphasis on gross margin recovery and recurring revenue keeps the main near term catalyst intact, while the biggest risk remains execution and customer concentration as orders ramp across a still choppy semiconductor cycle.

The most relevant update here is Cohu’s Q1 2026 sales guidance of about US$122 million, roughly flat with Q4. That suggests the strong order rebound and design wins discussed on the call may take time to show up in reported revenue, which matters if you see the high bandwidth memory and Eclipse handler ramps as key drivers for the story. data.title: Veidekke (OB:VEI) Valuation After Strong Earnings And Higher Proposed Dividend
data.description:

See our latest analysis for Veidekke.

The latest updates appear to have kept momentum intact, with a 16.77% 90 day share price return and a 33.75% 1 year total shareholder return supporting sentiment around the NOK 189.4 share price and the recent earnings and dividend news.

Preferred P/E of 16.5x: Is it justified?

On the latest numbers, Veidekke trades on a P/E of 16.5x, which screens as expensive compared to both its own fair P/E estimate and peer benchmarks.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price to earnings per share. It reflects how much investors are paying for each unit of current earnings. For a construction and infrastructure group like Veidekke, this often captures expectations around order book quality, margin stability and how durable current earnings might be across the cycle.

Here, the current 16.5x P/E sits above the estimated fair P/E of 14.7x. That level is the fair ratio estimate that the market could potentially gravitate toward if sentiment or fundamentals shift.
